issues.evidence: api.get('/assessments') called in Dashboard.jsx and Assessments.jsx but no assessments.py route file found in business/backend/routes/
issues.issue: Frontend calls /api/assessments but no matching backend route file exists. Will 404 in production. IMPORTANT: The boilerplate loader.py mounts routes/assessments.py at prefix /api/assessments. Route decorators must be relative to that prefix — use @router.get('/') not @router.get('/assessments/'). Using /assessments/ in the decorator creates double-path /api/assessments/assessments/ which 404s.
issues.fix: Create business/backend/routes/assessments.py with FastAPI APIRouter. Use these EXACT decorator paths (relative, no stem repeat): @router.get('/') for list, @router.post('/') for create, @router.get('/{assessment_id}') for get-one, @router.put('/{assessment_id}') for update, @router.delete('/{assessment_id}') for delete. Model fields are: id (Integer PK), business_name (String), industry (String), score (String), responses (Text). Import from models.Assessment import Assessment. All endpoints: db: Session = Depends(get_db), current_user: dict = Depends(get_current_user).

issues.evidence: reports.py uses @router.get('/reports/{assessment_id}') and @router.get('/reports/{assessment_id}/download') but loader.py already mounts this file at prefix /api/reports. Full paths become /api/reports/reports/{id} and /api/reports/reports/{id}/download. Frontend calls /api/reports/{id} — will 404.
issues.issue: Double-path routing bug: route decorators repeat the stem 'reports' that the boilerplate loader already uses as the mount prefix. Every call from the frontend 404s in production.
issues.fix: In business/backend/routes/reports.py, change the two route decorators: @router.get('/reports/{assessment_id}') → @router.get('/{assessment_id}') and @router.get('/reports/{assessment_id}/download') → @router.get('/{assessment_id}/download'). Keep all other code identical — only the decorator path strings change.

issues.evidence: Intake specifies PDF output (T005: 'Create PDF report template and generation', TV003: 'PDF report downloads with proper formatting') but no PDF generation library found and download endpoint returns application/json.
issues.issue: Intake requires downloadable PDF reports but no PDF library is present and the download endpoint returns JSON. The must-have feature is 'Downloadable executive report' and the acceptance test is a PDF download.
issues.fix: 1) Add `reportlab` to business/requirements.txt. 2) In reports.py download_report endpoint: import BytesIO from io and SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, Table from reportlab.platypus, getSampleStyleSheet from reportlab.lib.styles, A4 from reportlab.lib.pagesizes. Build a PDF with: title 'AI Workforce Intelligence Report', business_name, industry, generated_at, then a table of KPI scores (one row per KPI key/value from report_data['scores']), then recommendations as bullet paragraphs. Return StreamingResponse(BytesIO(pdf_bytes), media_type='application/pdf', headers={'Content-Disposition': f'attachment; filename=report_{assessment_id}.pdf'}). Remove the tempfile JSON approach entirely.
